title: Execution plan for research instrumentation proof case
objective: Turn the selected regression/instrumentation candidate into one bounded Phase-2A execution path with explicit inputs, review logic, intervention scope, and measurement criteria.

phases.name: Capture trigger evidence
phases.goal: Replace scaffold placeholders with a concrete triggering snapshot and provenance.
inputs_required: raw dashboard or metric snapshot reference
current success/quality definition used for the alert
done_when: signal object has no placeholder provenance fields
the alert can be traced back to specific underlying evidence

phases.name: Run review and classify the case
phases.goal: Create a review artifact that decides whether the case looks like true regression, measurement artifact, or remains uncertain.
done_when: review decision is recorded with rationale

phases.name: Define one minimal reversible intervention
phases.goal: Choose the smallest useful change, preferably diagnostic/measurement-first rather than routing/default mutation.
guardrails: no skill demotion from thin evidence
prefer instrumentation or segmentation change first
done_when: the intervention is narrow and reversible

phases.name: Implement and log the runtime change
phases.goal: Record exactly what changed, where, and when.
done_when: change can be audited and reversed

phases.name: Measure bounded post-change effect
phases.goal: Observe the case over one defined window and produce an honest verdict with confounders.
done_when: final verdict is one of: true-regression, measurement-artifact, inconclusive

open_dependencies: decision on whether bounded measurement is sufficient for local verdict
optional repeated observation window for stronger confidence
broader downstream evidence for sustained review-quality improvement
success_conditions: All five trace-spine objects have concrete non-placeholder owner/time/reference fields.
One reversible intervention is actually executed.
The result can be honestly classified without overselling.
